<templatestyles src="Template:User-JWBTH-Convenient Discussions/styles.css" />
<languages />
{{shortcut2|User:JWBTH/CD}}
<div style="width: fit-content; margin: 1em auto; text-align: center; font-size: 1.4em;">
<div class="ts-cdLogo">[[{{Localized media|Convenient Discussions logo color|svg}}|475px|<translate><!--T:1--> The script's logo. The colors represent the backgrounds of comments of different types</translate>]]</div>
<p style="margin-top: 0.25em;"><translate>Makes collaboration smooth and efficient on MediaWiki talk pages</translate></p>
</div>

<div style="width: fit-content; margin: 1em auto; display: flex; gap: 1.5em;">
[[{{Localized media|Convenient Discussions hero|png}}|thumb|x350px|none|<translate>Light mode + Spacious design + Relative time</translate>]]
[[{{Localized media|Convenient Discussions hero dark|png}}|thumb|x350px|none|<translate>Dark mode + Compact design + Absolute time</translate>]]
</div>
{{2021 Coolest tool award|Convenient Discussions|Versatile|float=right}}
<translate>
<!--T:4-->
'''Convenient Discussions''' ('''CD''') is a JavaScript tool that enhances MediaWiki talk pages, redesigning threads, adding more comment and section actions, new comment navigation, real-time updates, autocomplete, and dozens of other features to make wiki discussions smooth and efficient.

CD has been developed since 2017 by <tvar name="jack-link">[[User:Jack who built the house|Jack who built the house]]</tvar>. It is enriched by contributions and feedback from the global Wikimedia community and integrates solutions and ideas from the Wikimedia Foundation's engineering and design teams.

== Features == <!--T:96-->
</translate>
[[File:Convenient Discussions wikilinks autocomplete.png|thumb|<translate>Cross-wiki wikilinks autocomplete ([[:File:Convenient Discussions wikilinks autocomplete.gif|animation]]). {{key|Tab}} to insert the page name, {{key|Enter}} to finalize the markup. ''How can we promote cross-wiki collaboration if people can't even link pages on other wikis easily?''</translate>]]
<translate>
Predating the [[mw:Extension:DiscussionTools|DiscussionTools]] (DT) project, CD offers a wide range of features beyond DT functionality:

* Discussion threads are redesigned, making the hierarchy of replies clear
* <tvar name="user-icon"><span tabindex="0" class="ts-userInfoCardMock" style="top: -1px; position: relative; cursor: pointer; width: 24px; height: 24px; display: inline-flex; justify-content: center; border-width: 1px; border-style: solid; border-radius: 2px; border-color: transparent;"><span style="opacity: 0.65;">[[File:OOjs UI icon userAvatar.svg|16px|link=]]</span></span></tvar> '''Author''' and date appear ''at the top'' of each comment ''(opt-out)''
* You can edit comments, move sections, create subsections, and vote in polls
* New comments can be navigated using the [[#Navigation panel|navigation panel]] or the table of contents
** No more reading talk pages through diffs
* Comments are [[#Comment types|highlighted]] by type: <span class="ts-label ts-label-new" style="display: inline-block; background-color: #e8fff1; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #00af89;">new or updated</span>, <span class="ts-label ts-label-own" style="display: inline-block; background-color: #faf3fc; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #9f33cc;">your own</span>, <span class="ts-label ts-label-jumpedTo" style="display: inline-block; background-color: #fef6e7; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #edab00;">jumped-to</span>, <span class="ts-label ts-label-linked" style="display: inline-block; background-color: #e8eeff; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #36c;">linked</span>, <span class="ts-label ts-label-deleted" style="display: inline-block; background-color: #fee7e6; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #d73333;">deleted</span>
* The page checks for updates in real time:
** A live counter shows the number of new comments
** The table of contents and individual threads show which items have new activity and from whom
** Simple comment edits by other users are rendered on the spot, with a diff available
** [[w:Pop-up notification|Desktop notifications]] are available while the page is open
* Timestamps are shown in your local time in one of several formats, including relative time ''(opt-in)''
* Long, deeply nested discussions are handled well:
** Threads collapse automatically or on demand, making it easier to scan and navigate
** You can jump between a comment and its parent
** When replying to the opening comment of a topic, the reply form can be under it or at the end of the section
*** No need to scroll back and forth to reply or check details
** {{template|outdent}} templates are respected and inserted in a clever way so as not to break or confuse the reply hierarchy
* Beyond <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext">@mentions</syntaxhighlight>, there is autocomplete for <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext">[[#comment links]]</syntaxhighlight>, <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext">[[wikilinks]]</syntaxhighlight>, <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext">{{templates}}</syntaxhighlight>, and <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext"><tags></syntaxhighlight>
** Wikilinks autocomplete works across interwiki prefixes ''(see image)'' and will even let you autocomplete a page section on a distant wiki

Among other things, CD lets you:
* comment in several places at once, without closing other forms;
* quote comments with their formatting preserved;
* get pasted links to Wikimedia wikis converted to wikilinks
** <code><nowiki>http://fr.wiktionary.org/wiki/mot#Français</nowiki></code> on Commons becomes <syntaxhighlight inline lang="wikitext">[[wikt:fr:mot#Français]]</syntaxhighlight>
* use custom text snippets for anything you type frequently;
* upload screenshots to Commons with ease: paste → pick the source → upload;
* copy links in several formats;
* sort a topic's contributors by name, comment count, and date;
* jump straight to a comment from the watchlist, history, contributions, and elsewhere via a "(comment)" link.

CD is highly customizable [[#Usage|per-user]] and [[#Configuring for a wiki|per-wiki]], works across wikis, [https://translatewiki.net/wiki/Translating:Convenient_Discussions 43 languages], skins, themes, and browsers, supports RTL scripts and anonymous editing.

== Limitations ==
* Doesn't support [[mw:Extension:VisualEditor|VisualEditor]]
* Doesn't work in the mobile version (but works on mobile with the desktop view)

== Installation == <!--T:9-->

<!--T:10-->
To install the script for all Wikimedia wikis, add this to your [[<tvar name="my-global">meta:Special:MyPage/global.js</tvar>|global.js on Meta]]:
</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js" copy>
mw.loader.load('https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Jack_who_built_the_house/convenientDiscussions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript');
</syntaxhighlight>
<translate>
<!--T:11-->
To install the script on a specific wiki where it's not a gadget, add the same code to your common.js. If the wiki has the script available as a gadget, it makes sense to enable the gadget if you have the script installed globally—the script will load faster on that wiki.

<!--T:12-->
Every wiki has its own peculiarities that the script can address in the configuration file. Those are individual for each wiki and created by volunteers. If you know JavaScript and your wiki doesn't have one, see {{section link||Configuring for a wiki}}. Having a configuration file for the wiki will also speed up the execution of the script.

=== Compatibility === <!--T:13-->

</translate>
[[File:Convenient Discussion with deactivated timestamp settings.png|thumb|<translate><!--T:102--> Make sure to deactivate timestamp transforming by changing CD's timestamps settings to what's in the screenshot.</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:14-->
Convenient Discussions shows timestamps in local time by default. However, you can use another user script for this purpose. To make Convenient Discussions compatible with the script displaying time in the local time zone (<tvar name="comments-in-local-time">[[w:User:Gary/comments in local time.js]]</tvar> or <tvar name=2>[[w:User:Mxn/CommentsInLocalTime]]</tvar>), call the latter like this:
</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js" style="background: transparent;" copy>
mw.hook( 'convenientDiscussions.commentsReady' ).add( function () {
	// Import the script here
} );
</syntaxhighlight>
<translate>
<!--T:103-->
Note that this will not load the script if Convenient Discussions doesn't load. To make the script also load for pages Convenient Discussions won't load on, call it like this:
</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js" style="background: transparent;" copy>
if (
  mw.config.get( 'wgNamespaceNumber' ) % 2 !== 1 &&
  !mw.config.get( 'wgExtraSignatureNamespaces' ).includes( mw.config.get( 'wgNamespaceNumber' ) )
) {
	// Import the script here
}
</syntaxhighlight>
<translate>
<!--T:104-->
For Gary's script specifically, an additional line of code is needed to disable its feature of not loading twice:
</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js" style="background: transparent;" copy>
mw.hook( 'convenientDiscussions.commentsReady' ).add( function () {
	window.commentsInLocalTimeWasRun = false;
	mw.loader.load( '//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Gary/comments in local time.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript' ); // [[w:en:User:Mxn/CommentsInLocalTime]]
}
</syntaxhighlight>
<translate>
<!--T:95-->
Be sure to disable the "Comments in Local Time" gadget in your preferences if it is present in your wiki (e.g. the English Wikipedia).

== Usage == <!--T:15-->
</translate>
[[{{Localized media|Convenient Discussions settings - comment form|png}}|thumb|400px|<translate><!--T:90--> Settings dialog</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:16-->
You can adjust the script according to your preferences in the settings dialog. It is available at the click of the gear icon [[<tvar name="gear-icon">File:OOjs UI icon advanced.svg</tvar>|OOjs UI icon advanced]] which can be found in the "CD" section of the watchlist and under any comment form.

<!--T:17-->
The hotkeys can be found at the click of the "?" button under any comment form and on hover over the navigation panel buttons.

=== Comment types === <!--T:18-->
</translate>
<div style="overflow: hidden;">
* <div style="display: inline-block; background-color: #e8fff1; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #00af89;><translate><!--T:21--> New or updated comment</translate></div>
** A comment is considered new if the page was loaded within 15 minutes since the comment was loaded first time.
* <div style="display: inline-block; background-color: #faf3fc; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #9f33cc;"><translate><!--T:22--> Your own comment</translate></div>
* <div style="display: inline-block; background-color: #fef6e7; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #edab00;"><translate><!--T:20--> Jumped-to comment</translate></div>
** That's when you post a new comment or navigate to a comment using the [[#Navigation panel|navigation panel]], table of contents, section/comment header, or a link in another comment
* <div style="display: inline-block; background-color: #e8eeff; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #36c;"><translate>Linked comment</translate></div>
** That's when you open a page by a comment link, including <span style="white-space: nowrap;">"(comment)"</span> links and [[mw:Extension:Echo|Echo]] notifications
* <div style="display: inline-block; background-color: #fee7e6; padding: 0 5px; border-left: 3px solid #d73333;"><translate><!--T:89--> Deleted comment</translate></div>
</div>
<translate>

=== Navigation panel === <!--T:23-->
</translate>
<div style="margin-top: 1em;"></div>
[[{{Localized media|Convenient Discussions navigation panel info|svg}}]]
<translate>

== FAQ == <!--T:24-->

<!--T:25-->
; The script doesn't work on a page where it should work or vice versa. How to fix it?
: The script uses a number of factors to determine whether it should process a certain page, such as the presence of the "Add topic" tab and the <tvar name=wgExtraSignatureNamespaces><code>wgExtraSignatureNamespaces</code></tvar> config value. The simplest way to make it process/not process a page is to click "Run Convenient Discussions on this page once" in the page footer. But that will work only for one time. You should contact the user maintaining the configuration file on your wiki or create your own configuration file according to the [[#Configuring for a wiki|instructions below]]. In that file, [[w:en:Regular expression|regular expressions]] to match page names should be added to/removed from the <tvar name=pageWhitelist><code>pageWhitelist</code></tvar> / <tvar name=pageBlacklist><code>pageBlacklist</code></tvar> values. A worse way to deal with it is to add an element with <tvar name=talkPage><code>class="cd-talkPage"</code></tvar> or <tvar name=notTalkPage><code>class="cd-notTalkPage"</code></tvar> to the source code of the page. In that case, the page will still be classified incorrectly on log pages (the watchlist, history pages, etc.).

<!--T:26-->
; What does "(-)" after the comment text in an edit summary mean?
: It means that the whole text of the comment is displayed in the edit summary, and there is no more text in the comment other than that is displayed, so no point to open the page if you only want to see the contents of this edit. It's a notation that was used on some old Internet forums.

<!--T:28-->
; The comment menu (Reply/Edit/Thank/...) overlaps a user link, and I can't click it.
: Make a long click/tap or right click on the block; it will disappear.

<!--T:29-->
; How to set settings for one wiki, not for all wikis?
: Use <tvar name="local-code"><code>var cdLocal''SettingName'' = ''value'';</code></tvar> in your common.js on that wiki. You can get the names of the settings [<tvar name="github-boot">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/47636aacdb904c8ec7acb2d62abb2b7ec71fa8ef/src/js/settings.js#L67</tvar> here] (the first letter should be in upper case, for example <tvar name=autopreview><code>autopreview</code></tvar> → <tvar name=cdLocalAutopreview><code>cdLocalAutopreview</code></tvar>).

<!--T:30-->
; I would like to have <tvar name=colon1><code>:&nbsp;</code></tvar> inserted after a user mention like in the <tvar name="ping-template">{{tl|ping}}</tvar> template.
: Hold <tvar name=key1>{{key press|Alt}}</tvar> while choosing the name you want to mention. You can also press the mention icon while holding <tvar name=key2>{{key press|Alt}}</tvar> — if you're replying to a comment, a mention of the target comment's author with <tvar name=colon2><code>:&nbsp;</code></tvar> will appear at the beginning of your comment.

== Data == <!--T:31-->

<!--T:32-->
Here's what the script stores, why, how, and how to delete the data. Note that you can delete all the data associated with the script in one click, by opening the script settings and pressing the button "Remove all script data" on the "Data removal" tab. Note that while the settings (except for a few) are global, visits and watched sections are stored individually for each wiki, so you'll have to remove them separately.
</translate>
{| class="wikitable wide"
! <translate><!--T:33--> What</translate> !! <translate><!--T:34--> Why</translate> !! <translate><!--T:35--> How</translate> !! <translate><!--T:36--> How to delete</translate>{{ref|1}}
|-
| <translate><!--T:37--> Settings</translate>
| <translate><!--T:38--> To allow tuning the script according to the user's preferences.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:39--> On the Wikimedia servers as a user option named <tvar name="cd-settings"><code>userjs-convenientDiscussions-settings</code></tvar>.</translate>
|
<translate><!--T:40--> Global settings:</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js">
new mw.Api().postWithEditToken({
  action: 'globalpreferences',
  optionname: 'userjs-convenientDiscussions-settings',
});
</syntaxhighlight>
<translate><!--T:41--> Local settings:</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js">
new mw.Api().saveOption('userjs-convenientDiscussions-localSettings', null);
</syntaxhighlight>
|-
| <translate><!--T:42--> Last talk page visits</translate>
| <translate><!--T:43--> To detect new comments on talk pages.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:44--> In [[w:en:Lempel–Ziv–Welch|compressed]] form, on the Wikimedia servers as a user option named <tvar name="cd-visits"><code>userjs-convenientDiscussions-visits</code></tvar>.</translate>{{ref|2}}
| <syntaxhighlight lang="js">
new mw.Api().saveOption('userjs-convenientDiscussions-visits', null);
</syntaxhighlight>
|-
| <translate><!--T:45--> Watched sections</translate>
| <translate><!--T:46--> (Only when enabled in the settings instead of the standard topic subscription.) To show notifications and highlight comments on pages that list revisions: the watchlist, recent changes page, history pages, user contributions pages.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:47--> In compressed form, on the Wikimedia servers as a user option named <tvar name="cd-watchedSections"><code>userjs-convenientDiscussions-watchedSections</code></tvar>.</translate>{{ref|2}}
|
<translate><!--T:48--> Using the "Edit watched sections list" dialog accessible from the watchlist, or</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js">
new mw.Api().saveOption('userjs-convenientDiscussions-watchedSections', null);
</syntaxhighlight>
|-
| <translate><!--T:49--> Unsent comment forms' content</translate>
| <translate><!--T:50--> To restore comment drafts after page reloads, browser crashes, accidental jumps to a different page, etc.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:51--> For no longer than 60 days, in the browser's [<tvar name="docs-localStorage">https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/localStorage</tvar> local storage].</translate>
| <syntaxhighlight lang="js">
localStorage.removeItem('convenientDiscussions-commentForms');
</syntaxhighlight>
|-
| <translate><!--T:52--> Edits thanked via CD</translate>
| <translate><!--T:53--> To reflect in the interface that a comment has already been thanked for.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:54--> For no longer than 60 days, in the browser's local storage.</translate>
| <syntaxhighlight lang="js">
localStorage.removeItem('convenientDiscussions-thanks');
</syntaxhighlight>
|-
| <translate><!--T:92--> Seen comment changes</translate>
| <translate><!--T:93--> When a comment is updated, its new version is sometimes rendered immediately. If the user has seen the update, this fact is saved in the memory, so that there is no notification the next time the user sees it.</translate>
| <translate><!--T:94--> For no longer than 60 days, in the browser's local storage.</translate>
| <syntaxhighlight lang="js">
localStorage.removeItem('convenientDiscussions-seenRenderedChanges');
</syntaxhighlight>
|}
# {{note|1}}<translate><!--T:57--> To execute the code in the "How to delete" column, open the browser's developer tools (done by pressing <tvar name=f12>{{key press|F12}}</tvar> in most browsers), switch to the "Console" tab, paste the code into the input and press <tvar name=enter>{{key press|Enter}}</tvar>.</translate>
# {{note|2}}<translate><!--T:55--> In Russian Wikipedia, for historical reasons, the option names use <tvar name="cd-name"><code>cd</code></tvar> instead of <tvar name="convenientDiscussions-name"><code>convenientDiscussions</code></tvar>, and <tvar name="cd-watchedTopics"><code>userjs-cd-watchedTopics</code></tvar> is used for the watched sections option.</translate>

<translate>
<!--T:56-->
Note that other scripts that you use on wiki pages, as well as side-wide scripts, have access to this data too.

== Feedback == <!--T:58-->

<!--T:59-->
It's best to post bug reports and proposals on Phabricator under [<tvar name="phab-project">https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/tag/convenient-discussions/</tvar> the "convenient-discussions" tag]. If you don't have an account there and don't want to create one, post to the [[<tvar name="talk-page">User talk:Jack who built the house/Convenient Discussions</tvar>|talk page]].

== Configuring for a wiki == <!--T:60-->
</translate>
[[File:Convenient Discussions project structure.svg|thumb|595px|<translate><!--T:61--> Structure of the project</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:62-->
The scheme of loading the script on a wiki is the following: There is [[<tvar name="script-page">User:Jack who built the house/convenientDiscussions.js</tvar>|the main script file]] on Commons and a configuration file on the wiki (if somebody has created it). The configuration file can be a gadget or a user script (a gadget will load faster).
* The configuration file requests the main script file if it is not loaded yet.
* Conversely, if the main file is loaded first, it requests the configuration file if the URL of the configuration file is specified [<tvar name="github-urls">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/main/config/urls.json</tvar> in it].
So, there is no difference which file is loaded first.

<!--T:63-->
To configure the script for a wiki, you may use the configuration generator as described below and then supplement it with configuration values you want. If you want the configuration file to be loaded when users load the script from Commons, you should contact the [<tvar name="github-repo">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions</tvar> repository] [[<tvar name="jack-page">User:Jack who built the house</tvar>|owner]]. If it proves safe, he will add the URL of the configuration to [<tvar name="github-urls">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/master/config/urls.json</tvar> config/urls.json] (you can make a pull request too).

<!--T:83-->
To generate a configuration, run '''[[<tvar name="generator-script">User:Jack who built the house/convenientDiscussions-generateBasicConfig.js</tvar>|the generator script]]''' in your browser's console on any page of the wiki you want the configuration for. You can do it by executing <tvar name="generator-script-code"><syntaxhighlight lang="js" copy>mw.loader.load('https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Jack_who_built_the_house/convenientDiscussions-generateBasicConfig.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript');</syntaxhighlight></tvar> in the console. The result will contain a configuration object with values that can be automatically retrieved (system messages, some site info, template names) wrapped in the universal wrapping code. You then should create a configuration file with that content.

<!--T:84-->
The meaningful part of the configuration is contained in the <tvar name="config-prop"><code>convenientDiscussions.config</code></tvar> object. Its possible properties are documented [<tvar name="docs-defaultConfig">https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/module-defaultConfig.html</tvar> here] with default values, alphabetically. [<tvar name="github-defaultConfig">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/master/config/default.js</tvar> The source code] of that documentation has them in a more logical order.

<!--T:75-->
It is recommended to keep only those properties that differ from the default ones.

<!--T:85-->
You can also add any additional instuctions (for example hooks) you need below the object.

=== Examples === <!--T:76-->
</translate>
* [[w:en:User:Jack who built the house/convenientDiscussions.js]] ([https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/master/config/w-en.js <translate><!--T:87--> source in the repository</translate>])
* [[w:ru:MediaWiki:Gadget-convenientDiscussions.js]] ([https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/master/config/w-ru.js <translate><!--T:88--> source in the repository</translate>])

<translate>
=== How do I turn off the "Reply" buttons? === <!--T:98-->
</translate>
{{see also|mw:Help:DiscussionTools/Magic words and markup}}
<translate>
<!--T:99-->
To prevent the "Reply" buttons and the rest of the comment layout from showing up in certain templates used on talk pages (e.g. in the "Moved discussion" template after the mover's signature), add the <tvar name="mw-notalk"><code>mw-notalk</code></tvar> class to those templates.

<!--T:100-->
To prevent the "Reply" buttons from showing up in closed discussions, add the <tvar name="mw-archivedtalk"><code>mw-archivedtalk</code></tvar> class to the template.

=== Debugging using Node.js and Git === <!--T:86-->

<!--T:65-->
If you want to debug your configuration more closely in the browser's development tools and you're familiar with [[w:en:Node.js|Node.js]] and [[w:en:Git|Git]] and have them installed, clone [<tvar name="github-repo">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions</tvar> the script's repository], run <tvar name="npm-install"><code>npm install</code></tvar> while in the script's directory to install the dependencies, create a <tvar name="js-ext"><code>.js</code></tvar> file in the <tvar name="config-folder"><code>config</code></tvar> folder named using pattern <tvar name="config-pattern"><code>''project_code''[-''language_code''].js</code></tvar> (for example, <tvar name="w-en"><code>w-en.js</code></tvar>, <tvar name="wikt-de"><code>wikt-de.js</code></tvar>, <tvar name=mw><code>mw.js</code></tvar>), and put the following code into it:</translate>
<syntaxhighlight lang="js">
export default {
  // List of properties
};

// Any additional code, e.g. hooks
</syntaxhighlight>

<translate>
<!--T:66-->
Then you will need to add the code as described above. After the configuration is ready, you will need to build an actual configuration file that you will put in your wiki. Do it by running <tvar name="npm-run-configs"><code>npm run configs</code></tvar>. The resulting file will be named <tvar name="dist-config"><code>dist/convenientDiscussions-config/''filename''</code></tvar>.

<!--T:91-->
Test your configuration file by running <tvar name="npm-run-single"><code>npm run single -- --project=''project_code'' --lang=''language_code''</code></tvar> (use <code>-- -- --project</code> in PowerShell). A file named <tvar name="serve-url-single"><code>convenientDiscussions.single.''project_code''[-''language_code''].js</code></tvar> will be created (and updated on code changes) in the <tvar name=dist><code>dist</code></tvar> folder. Load it to the wiki using <tvar name="load-localhost-single"><code>mw.loader.load('<nowiki>http://</nowiki>localhost:9000/''filename''<nowiki />')</code></tvar>.

<!--T:68-->
It will be best if you make a pull request to include your configuration in the CD repository. This way, if something in the script environment changes that affects your configuration file (the format of a property changes, for instance, or a default value, that your value is based on, is updated), other people including the maintainer of the script would be able to notice it and inform you and/or make changes themselves.

== Development == <!--T:77-->

<!--T:78-->
* [<tvar name="github-repo">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions</tvar> GitHub repository] is used to store code. GitHub Actions is used to build and deploy the resulting files to wikis, as well as docs to Toolforge.
* [<tvar name="phabricator-project">https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/tag/convenient-discussions/</tvar> Phabricator tag] is used to coordinate efforts. (Post bug reports and proposals there, not at GitHub.)
* [<tvar name="toolforge-docs">https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/</tvar> Toolforge] has automatically generated code documentation ''for older versions''.
** Starting from v8.0.0, the project relies on TypeScript-flavored JSDoc which makes the project type-safe but doesn't let autogenerate documentation.
* [<tvar name=translatewiki>https://translatewiki.net/wiki/Translating:Convenient_Discussions</tvar> Translatewiki] has localization strings. Please suggest improvements to the English source through a pull request to [<tvar name="github-en">https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/main/i18n/en.json</tvar> en.json].

<!--T:79-->
Your contributions are welcome! You can either improve the script itself or write plugins for it. Some notes:
* The global object of the script is <tvar name="script-object">[https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/module-cd-convenientDiscussions.html <code>convenientDiscussions</code>]</tvar> (the modules use the <tvar name="cd-alias"><code>cd</code></tvar> alias).
* <tvar name="cd-strings-link"><code>[https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/convenientDiscussions.html#.s convenientDiscussions.s()]</code></tvar> is an analog of <tvar name="mw-strings"><code>mw.msg()</code></tvar> for the script's language strings. <tvar name="cd-strings-parse-link"><code>[https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/convenientDiscussions.html#.sParse convenientDiscussions.sParse()]</code></tvar> is an analog of <tvar name="mw-strings-parse"><code>mw.message(...).parse()</code></tvar>. Please make sure all strings that have their raw HTML inserted into the page use <tvar name="cd-strings-parse"><code>convenientDiscussions.sParse()</code></tvar> to prevent introducing [[w:en:XSS|XSS]] vulnerabilities. (All code from untrusted sources is sanitized at earlier stages, but a double check won't hurt.)
* The "events" in the left panel of the [<tvar name="toolforge-docs">https://tools-static.wmflabs.org/convenient-discussions/docs/</tvar> documentation] correspond to the names used by <tvar name="mw-hook"><code>mw.hook</code></tvar>. For example, to attach a handler to the <tvar name=commentFormCreated><code>commentFormCreated</code></tvar> event, you need the code <tvar name="mw-hook-commentFormCreated"><code>mw.hook('convenientDiscussions.commentFormCreated').add(''handler'');</code></tvar>.
* To see message names instead of messages themselves on a page, add the ''<tvar name="uselang-qqx">uselang=qqx</tvar>'' parameter to the end of the URL (just like with MediaWiki).
* If you write a plugin and need some internal method to be available publicly via the global object, contact the [[<tvar name="jack-page">User:Jack who built the house</tvar>|script's maintainer]] (or just make a relevant pull request).
* To test, run <code>npm run test</code>. Only several key modules are tested. Parser tests are documented in [https://github.com/jwbth/convenient-discussions/blob/main/tests/parser-tests.md tests/parser-tests.md].

=== Building === <!--T:80-->

<!--T:81-->
Use:
* <tvar name="npm-run-start"><code>npm run dev</code></tvar> to serve the script from <tvar name="serve-url"><nowiki>http://localhost:9000/src/loader/startup.js</nowiki></tvar> and automatically rebuild on updates. Wiki configuration and translation will still be loaded from web.
* <tvar name="npm-run-single"><code>npm run single -- --project=''project_code'' --lang=''language_code''</code></tvar> (use <code>-- -- --project</code> in PowerShell) to serve the script as a single file. Useful to debug configuration and translation.
* <tvar name="npm-run-build"><code>npm run build</code></tvar> to build the main file, source maps, [[#Configuring for a wiki|project configuration]] files, translation files, and license file. Use <tvar name="test-param1"><code>--staging</code></tvar> to build the files with the <tvar name="test-postfix1"><code>.staging</code></tvar> postfix.
* <tvar name="npm-run-configs"><code>npm run configs</code></tvar> to generate [[#Configuring for a wiki|wiki configuration]] files. Use <tvar name="test-param2"><code>--staging</code></tvar> to generate the files with the <tvar name="test-postfix2"><code>.staging</code></tvar> postfix.
* <tvar name="npm-run-deploy"><code>npm run deploy</code></tvar> to deploy the built files (including wiki configurations) to wikis as configured in <tvar name=json5><code>config.json5</code></tvar>. Use <tvar name="test-param3"><code>--staging</code></tvar> to deploy the staging versions.

== See also == <!--T:82-->
</translate>
* [[:mw:Extension:DiscussionTools|Extension:DiscussionTools]]
* [[:mw:Extension:StructuredDiscussions|Extension:StructuredDiscussions]] (Flow)

[[Category:Convenient Discussions]]
